expectations.text: states-situation-plainly: The draft describes the situation in factual, neutral terms without emotional language or blame
expectations.evidence: Opening: 'We've been waiting 10 days on the Payments team for the updated webhook spec... I've pinged Sasha three times on Slack and sent an email last Thursday. No response so far.' — purely factual, no charged language.

expectations.text: includes-impact: The draft mentions the concrete impact (sprint deadline, integration risk, or standup accountability)
expectations.evidence: 'Our sprint ends Friday. Without the spec, we can't finish the integration, and that work will slip out of the sprint. Our PM is going to ask about it tomorrow' — concrete deadline and accountability loop both present.

expectations.text: references-prior-attempts: The draft mentions that attempts were already made to resolve directly
expectations.evidence: 'I've pinged Sasha three times on Slack and sent an email last Thursday.' — specific channels and frequency documented.

expectations.text: clear-ask: The draft includes a specific ask — either from Keiko to intervene or a concrete next step
expectations.evidence: 'I need either the spec by Wednesday so we have time to integrate, or your help getting Sasha's manager looped in to unblock this.' — binary, actionable ask with a decision point.

expectations.text: no-blamey-language: The draft avoids language that attacks Sasha or the Payments team
expectations.evidence: 'No response so far' is neutral. Notes explicitly say 'she may just be swamped or unclear on the priority.' No blame assigned.
